title: Buttery Dense Thai Pumpkin Stir-fry
description: A hangover-curing dish featuring dark rum and blanched pumpkin, ideal for pescatarians seeking a buttery and dense meal.
instructions: Blanch the pumpkin in boiling water for about 1 minute, then plunge into ice water to stop cooking.
In a skillet over medium heat, melt butter and sauté garlic until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add blanched pumpkin pieces to the skillet, tossing gently with salt and dark rum. Stir-fry until well combined, ensuring no clumps form.
If using Thai chili peppers, add them now; stir for an additional minute before serving.
Garnish with fresh herbs if desired.
